chapter_title.ar: باب فَضْلِ ارْتِبَاطِ الْخَيْلِ وَ إِجْرَائِهَا وَ الرَّمْيِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 22 – Merits of bridling the cavalry horses and injuring them, and the throwing (Archery)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/390)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/390)
text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) ارْكَبُوا وَ ارْمُوا وَ إِنْ تَرْمُوا أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ أَنْ تَرْكَبُوا

ثُمَّ قَالَ كُلُّ لَهْوِ الْمُؤْمِنِ بَاطِلٌ إِلَّا فِي ثَلَاثٍ فِي تَأْدِيبِهِ الْفَرَسَ وَ رَمْيِهِ عَنْ قَوْسِهِ وَ مُلَاعَبَتِهِ امْرَأَتَهُ فَإِنَّهُنَّ حَقٌّ أَلَا إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَيُدْخِلُ فِي السَّهْمِ الْوَاحِدِ الثَّلَاثَةَ الْجَنَّةَ عَامِلَ الْخَشَبَةِ وَ الْمُقَوِّيَ بِهِ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَ الرَّامِيَ بِهِ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Ali Bin Ismail, raising it, said,
‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘Ride (horses) and throw (arrows – i.e., archery), and that archery is more beloved to me<sup>saww</sup> than if your ride (horses)’.

Then he<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘Every amusement of the Believer is false except regarding three – disciplining the horse, and throwing (arrows) from his bow, and his playing with his wife, for these are rightful. Indeed! Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic would Enter (each) one of the three into Paradise with regards to the arrow – the maker of the wood, and the one who strengthens it in the Way of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and the shooter with it in the Way of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 12. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from ‘Imran ibn Musa‘ from al-Hassan ibn Tarif from ‘Abd Allah ibn al-Mughirah in a marfu’ manner the following: “About the words of Allah, ‘Prepare and maintain whatever power and horses you can. . . .’ [8:60] the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, has said, “It is a reference to sharp-shooting.”
